prediction.reasoning: The point estimate is based on the current price, assuming a random walk for short-term predictions without strong directional signals. The confidence intervals are calculated using a log-normal distribution model, which is common for stock prices. We used an estimated annualized volatility of 20% for AMGN, derived from historical data. This volatility was then scaled by the square root of time (18 days out of 252 trading days) to determine the expected standard deviation of returns over the 18-day period. Standard Z-scores were applied to this standard deviation to construct the specified confidence intervals.
